ContentData.description: General content record for various data formats. Visibility is enforced by the generated ThorAPI service/ACL path for both list and single object reads. Public discoverability for published article/blog content uses the standard generated list contract: GET /v1/ContentData?example={urlEncodedJson}, with QBE fields such as {"status":"published","category":"BLOG","contentType":"markdown"}. The list response is always filtered by owner/admin/explicit READ ACL, so private or draft rows are omitted rather than exposed through a separate public-content route. Slug URLs resolve through the generated ContentData slug lookup and then the canonical UUID read path.

SemanticIndexEntry.description: Central GrayMatter semantic index row. This stores vector embeddings and compressed recall metadata in the RDB object graph while keeping embeddings outside the normalized source business tables.  Agents query this table by indexed object reference, owner/org/tenant scope, model, and status, then lazy-load the source object and graph relationships. They do not scan database schemas.  Referential integrity model: - targetType + targetId is the polymorphic object reference in the relational graph - principal, organization, and memoryEntry are real FK lookup accelerators for common scopes - tenantScope is tenant/deployment isolation metadata, not a database-schema search surface - lifecycle services must mark rows stale/deleted when the source object changes

sourceHash.description: SHA-256 hash of normalized source text used to skip unchanged reindexing.

summaryText.description: Compressed summary used for low-token context assembly.

keywordsText.description: Normalized lexical keywords for hybrid symbolic/semantic retrieval.

compressionStrategy.description: Context compression strategy applied before indexing and retrieval assembly.
